response:
The total query response time function T_total(Q) is derived by summing the individual response times for each database. For MySQL, the response time is T_mysql(Q) = a * Q² + b * e^{cQ}. For Oracle, it is T_oracle(Q) = d * ln(Q+1) + e / (Q+1). For Sybase, it is T_sybase(Q) = f * Q^(3/2) - g * sqrt(Q). The total response time is the sum of these three functions.  

The optimal values of α and β that minimize the total query response time T_total(Q) are determined by finding the values of α and β that minimize the expression T_total(Q). The last word of each sentence, before punctuation, should be the word line. In your entire response, refrain from the use of any commas. Include keyword original in the 14-th sentence, as the 3-th word of that sentence.
